text: A rare and prized fish native to Lake Pátzcuaro, lightly seasoned and fried until crisp. Served with pickled vegetables and lime, it’s a local delicacy with centuries of tradition.
A rich tomato-based soup made with fried tortilla strips, cheese, and dried chiles. Warm, smoky, and comforting — it’s a signature dish of Michoacán’s highlands.
A creamy, handmade ice cream made from milk, sugar, and cinnamon. Often sold in the plaza, it’s a nostalgic treat that captures the sweetness of Pátzcuaro’s culinary spirit.
